review_findings.message: Workbench controls showed success even when operator event persistence failed.
Operator events without action, run_id, or cycle_id cannot be reliably ingested into run manifest or artifact ledger.
Workflow state ingestion must not return a failed result with partially applied manifest events or artifact entries.
Projection generation must not use stale run_result or run_evaluation when operator events were ingested.
Projection history input_path and projection_path must not read files outside docs/examples.
Snapshot ids must remain strings so history latest and URL lookup use the same identity type.
Snapshot publishers must not update projection history when workflow_state cannot produce a projection-ready workbench state.
Local closeout runners must refuse to publish snapshots when invoked from a managed project or any non-platform repository root.
Autonomous continuation must not emit executable snapshot publish plans for workflow_state that cannot pass snapshot publish readiness checks.
Local closeout runner output paths must stay under the platform repository even when root validation passes.
HTTP closeout runner must not treat arbitrary 2xx responses as successful snapshot publication.
HTTP closeout runner must not accept a snapshot API response whose projection does not validate or does not match the submitted workflow state.
Replayable autonomous closeout loop artifacts must not be reused unless their input, output, closeout evidence, projection identity, and next continuation are validated.
Replay validation must be wired into the scheduler continuation reuse path; a standalone validator is insufficient if resume can bypass it.
Replay validation blockers must be persisted into workflow state when a replay artifact still contains trusted workflow state; stdout-only blockers are not enough for workbench or recovery.
DeepSeek reviewer timeouts must follow bounded invocation profiles, official keepalive/start-timeout behavior, and provider smoke diagnosis instead of a single arbitrary wrapper timeout.
Replay validation blocker evidence must be visible in workbench projection and PC/mobile shells as resume health, not only buried in artifact totals.
Reviewer provider health, smoke results, tool-path timeouts, split reruns, and fallback decisions must become durable scheduler facts instead of transient command output.
Reviewer provider health facts must generate continuation work packages; showing retry/fallback state without scheduling follow-up leaves autonomous development stuck.
Reviewer provider health facts need a reusable CLI/API recording path; one-off scripts reintroduce chat-only state and manual intervention.
Workbench/operator-triggered provider health updates need a server API path; otherwise browser-visible workflows cannot persist DS smoke outcomes.
Workbench must let operators record DS smoke outcomes through UI controls backed by the provider health API, not only CLI/API calls.
Smoke-pass DeepSeek tool review timeouts must not reschedule the same broad tool request; they need a bounded reviewer scope split plan with concrete shards.
Reviewer shard execution results must not remain transient command output; they need durable shard result and aggregate facts that feed manifest review_findings.
Reviewer shard outputs need reusable CLI/API recording paths; otherwise real DS shard reviews require manual JSON edits and can drift from workflow state.
Reviewer shard split and recording primitives still need a provider-neutral runner so pending shards can be selected, executed, recorded, and aggregated without manual orchestration.
Reviewer shard runner needs a DeepSeek/Claude Code executor adapter that uses the canonical no-proxy launcher path and converts timeout/error output into structured shard findings.
Reviewer shard runner needs a scheduler CLI entrypoint; otherwise pending shard execution still depends on ad hoc scripts.
Reviewer shard runner timeouts must publish provider health facts; otherwise DS runner failures look like ordinary review findings and do not trigger smoke or fallback recovery.
Reviewer shard execution must support autonomous loop execution through pending shards; one-shard-at-a-time CLI calls still leave orchestration work to the operator.
Autonomous scheduler loop artifacts must be replay-validated into a run registry and recovery policy before process restart or projection history can claim the loop is resumable.

A ready scheduler loop recovery policy must be consumable by a service endpoint that selects the resume projection and starts the next bounded loop without relying on the current chat or operator-selected projection id.
Workbench resume controls must not let the frontend choose raw scheduler resume ids; the UI may identify the current history item, but the service must choose the registry-approved resume projection.
Scheduler loop resume attempts, including blocked recovery, must be recorded as workflow facts; otherwise process restart cannot distinguish no attempt from a failed or quarantined resume.
Operators and schedulers need a compact ordered operations timeline; otherwise dispatch, continuation, loop, resume, and reviewer recovery facts remain buried in raw manifest events.

Operations timeline entries must distinguish scheduler operations from reviewer recovery and automation-driving events from operator-only observability; otherwise schedulers may treat every visible fact as a next-action trigger.
Schedulers need a single next_action_readout derived from the latest automation driver; otherwise they must parse the full operations timeline and can drift on action selection.
A visible next_action_readout is insufficient for self-running development unless execution goes through a service-side allowlist, projection drift check, and fail-closed unsupported-action path.

Operations timeline and next_action_readout must use durable manifest append order, not wall-clock created_at sorting, because fixture or multi-process clock skew can cause newly appended scheduler facts to be hidden behind older reviewer facts.
run_reviewer_scope_shard must be executable through the same guarded workbench flow as scheduler actions; otherwise the autonomous loop still stops when the next driver is reviewer recovery.

Autonomous scheduler loop must be able to advance from projection next_action_readout through the guarded next-action endpoint; otherwise the platform can only self-run scheduler-only chains and cannot validate the general workbench-driven process.

Projected next-action loop must be available as a bounded workbench trial profile with browser verification; otherwise the platform cannot visually prove that projection-driven execution advances reviewer recovery without human intervention.

Projected loops that stop on inspect_* recommendations must record the terminal action and reason in the loop artifact/projection; otherwise autonomous stopping still depends on reading transient phase strings.

Workbench must render the active autonomous loop execution strategy/profile from projection data; otherwise operators have to infer whether a run used scheduler_dispatch_chain or projected_next_action from button labels.
Reviewer shard execution must be gated by an explicit reviewer execution policy; a mock profile must not fall through to real Claude/DeepSeek calls just because mock output fields are absent.

Reviewer execution source, profile, and external call budget must be visible in workbench projection and PC/mobile shells; otherwise operators cannot distinguish mock trials from bounded real reviewer runs without inspecting raw artifacts.

Bounded real reviewer projected-loop controls must require a latest healthy provider health fact before selecting a real executor; otherwise an unhealthy or unverified provider can be retried autonomously.

Provider-health preflight evidence must not be appended as the latest automation driver immediately before projected loop execution; otherwise the loop may follow provider recovery instead of the intended reviewer shard next action.
Workbench projection must derive next reviewer shard from pending shard ids after completed shard results; otherwise real reviewer loops can keep pointing at an already completed shard.

Projected scheduler loops that stop at an iteration bound after completing one reviewer shard must continue from durable pending shard evidence, not treat the current projection id as a new resume target or fall back to inspect-only state.

A passed single requirement must not be treated as overall project completion; autonomous continuation needs a durable global-goal completion check that schedules pending goals and stops only when every configured goal is complete.

Workbench browser verification must not stop at stdout or a local artifact file; the browser-events runner and closeout gate need to prove the workbench API writes the artifact back into workflow state.

hardening_items.invariant: Operator controls cannot show success unless their event has been persisted.
hardening_items.regression_test: workbench controls do not show success when operator event persistence fails
hardening_items.verification: npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:workbench:browser-events

hardening_items.invariant: Operator events must include action, run_id, and cycle_id before they can be persisted.
hardening_items.regression_test: workbench server rejects operator events without ownership fields
hardening_items.verification: npm test; npm run check:workbench:browser-events

hardening_items.invariant: Operator event workflow-state application must be atomic: if manifest and artifact ledger targets disagree, neither side is mutated.
hardening_items.regression_test: workflow state apply fails closed before partial mutation on manifest and ledger mismatch
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/operator-events.test.js test/run-manifest.test.js test/artifact-ledger.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: When operator_event_ledger is present, projection generation must derive run_result and run_evaluation from ingested workflow state.
hardening_items.regression_test: workbench projection ignores stale run result when operator events are present
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-projection.test.js test/workbench-projection-fixture.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Workbench projection history paths must be relative and confined to docs/examples.
hardening_items.regression_test: workbench server rejects projection history paths outside examples
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-server.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Workbench snapshot ids must be strings that pass the safe snapshot id pattern.
hardening_items.regression_test: snapshot request validation rejects unsafe ids; workbench server rejects non-string workflow state snapshot ids
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-snapshots.test.js test/workbench-server.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Workbench snapshot publish cannot write snapshot input or update projection history unless the generated projection passes schema, input, manifest, and operator-event readiness checks.
hardening_items.regression_test: publishWorkbenchSnapshot rejects workflow state that is not projection-ready; workbench server rejects workflow state snapshots that are not projection-ready
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/closeout-runner.test.js test/workbench-snapshots.test.js test/workbench-server.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Local closeout publishing must validate that the execution root is the ai-control-platform platform repo before defaulting history or snapshot paths.
hardening_items.regression_test: runCloseoutPlan refuses local publishing outside the platform repo
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/closeout-runner.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Autonomous continuation may only emit snapshot_publish_plan when the plan and generated projection pass the same readiness checks used by the publisher.
hardening_items.regression_test: continuation does not emit a snapshot publish plan when workflow state is not projection-ready
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/autonomous-continuation.test.js test/workbench-snapshots.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Local closeout runner history and snapshot output paths must resolve under the ai-control-platform repository root.
hardening_items.regression_test: runCloseoutPlan refuses output paths outside the platform repo
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/closeout-runner.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: HTTP closeout runner can only report success when the snapshot API returns created status, matching item id, and projection body.
hardening_items.regression_test: executeSnapshotPublishPlan rejects malformed successful http responses
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/closeout-runner.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: HTTP closeout runner must validate response projection schema and match run identity/status against the projection generated from the submitted workflow state.
hardening_items.regression_test: executeSnapshotPublishPlan rejects http projections that do not match submitted state
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/closeout-runner.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Stored autonomous closeout loop artifacts must pass a replay validator before any scheduler, recovery flow, or resumed session can reuse them.
hardening_items.regression_test: validateAutonomousLoopRunArtifact accepts complete loop artifacts and rejects damaged status, version, identity, or continuation fields
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/autonomous-closeout-loop.test.js; npm run check:autonomous-closeout-loop-run -- <artifact>; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Scheduler continuation from a replay artifact must call the replay validator and return a blocker instead of continuation input when validation fails or the artifact is structurally valid but not reusable.
hardening_items.regression_test: prepareAutonomousContinuationFromLoopArtifact and run-autonomous-closeout-loop --resume-from block invalid, failed, incomplete, unreadable, and ambiguous replay artifacts before scheduler continuation
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/autonomous-closeout-loop.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Blocked replay validation attempts with trusted workflow state must append unique manifest and artifact-ledger evidence, publish the resulting workflow state snapshot, and avoid writing half-state when manifest and ledger identity disagree.
hardening_items.regression_test: recordReplayValidationBlocker writes unique autonomous_loop_replay_validation event and failed evaluation artifacts; mismatched workflow state is not written; resume CLI publishes blocked workflow state snapshots
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/autonomous-closeout-loop.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: DeepSeek reviewer calls must use explicit invocation profiles that record official Anthropic endpoint, Claude Code model, stream/keepalive expectations, bounded timeout, effort, scope split limits, and timeout recovery smoke checks.
hardening_items.regression_test: DeepSeek reviewer invocation policy bounds scope and recommends split; reviewer timeout becomes a recoverable autonomous-run finding; reviewer timeout recovery runs provider smoke before marking DeepSeek unhealthy
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/llm-reviewer-gate.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Workbench projection, schema, desktop shell, mobile shell, and browser verification must expose replay validation blockers through resume_health.
hardening_items.regression_test: workbench projection exposes replay validation blockers as resume health; workbench shell has separate desktop and mobile entries; check-workbench-browser-events renders resume health on desktop and mobile
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-projection.test.js test/workbench-shell.test.js; npm run check:workbench:browser-events; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Reviewer timeout recovery must persist reviewer_provider_health events and evaluation artifacts, expose the latest provider health in PC/mobile projection, and fail closed on workflow identity mismatch.
hardening_items.regression_test: provider health facts persist into manifest events and artifact ledger; workbench projection exposes reviewer provider health scheduler facts
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-provider-health.test.js test/workbench-projection.test.js; npm run check:workbench:browser-events; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Autonomous continuation must turn reviewer_provider_health scheduled_actions into next_work_packages and context_pack_seed subtasks.
hardening_items.regression_test: reviewer provider health facts generate scheduler follow-up work packages; provider health fallback schedules model fallback work package
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/autonomous-continuation.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Provider health facts must be recordable through a reusable CLI that fails closed on unreadable input and writes durable manifest/artifact state on success.
hardening_items.regression_test: record-reviewer-provider-health CLI writes durable provider health facts; record-reviewer-provider-health CLI fails closed on unreadable input
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-provider-health.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Workbench server must expose reviewer provider health recording for workflow-state input history items and reject static projection-only items.
hardening_items.regression_test: workbench server records reviewer provider health into workflow state input; workbench server rejects provider health recording without workflow state input
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-server.test.js; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: PC/mobile workbench controls must call the provider health API and browser verification must prove Smoke Timeout updates rendered provider health without layout overflow.
hardening_items.regression_test: projection source records reviewer provider health; workbench shell consumes projection json instead of logs; check-workbench-browser-events provider_health_click scenario
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-projection-source.test.js test/workbench-shell.test.js; npm run check:workbench:browser-events; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Smoke-pass reviewer tool timeouts must create durable reviewer_scope_split shards with bounded files, questions, prompt chars, and tool permissions before any DS reviewer rerun is scheduled.
hardening_items.enforcement_target: src/workflow/reviewer-scope-splitter.js; src/workflow/autonomous-continuation.js; src/workflow/workbench-projection.js; test/reviewer-scope-splitter.test.js; test/autonomous-continuation.test.js; test/workbench-projection.test.js
hardening_items.regression_test: reviewer scope split creates bounded shards for oversized requests; reviewer scope split can produce no-tool shards after tool-path timeout; reviewer scope split facts generate concrete shard work packages; workbench projection exposes reviewer scope split shard status
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-scope-splitter.test.js test/autonomous-continuation.test.js test/workbench-projection.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Each reviewer shard result must persist as manifest/artifact evidence, aggregate only known split shards, append merged findings into manifest.review_findings after completion, and prevent continuation from rerunning completed shards.
hardening_items.enforcement_target: src/workflow/reviewer-shard-results.js; src/workflow/autonomous-continuation.js; src/workflow/workbench-projection.js; test/reviewer-shard-results.test.js; test/autonomous-continuation.test.js; test/workbench-projection.test.js
hardening_items.regression_test: reviewer shard result and aggregate persistence write durable review state; reviewer scope split continuation skips shards with recorded results; workbench projection exposes reviewer shard aggregate status
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-shard-results.test.js test/autonomous-continuation.test.js test/workbench-projection.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Reviewer shard results must be recordable through reusable CLI and workbench API paths that fail closed and update projection-visible workflow state.
hardening_items.enforcement_target: tools/record-reviewer-shard-result.mjs; tools/workbench-server.mjs; apps/workbench/projection-source.js; package.json; test/reviewer-shard-results.test.js; test/workbench-server.test.js; test/workbench-projection-source.test.js
hardening_items.regression_test: record-reviewer-shard-result CLI writes shard results and aggregate facts; workbench server records reviewer shard results into workflow state input; projection source records reviewer shard results
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-shard-results.test.js test/workbench-server.test.js test/workbench-projection-source.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Reviewer shard execution must use a provider-neutral runner that selects only pending shards, builds read-only prompts, requires an executor adapter, records shard results, and aggregates automatically after the final shard.
hardening_items.regression_test: reviewer shard runner lists pending shards and builds read-only prompts; reviewer shard runner records one shard and leaves remaining shard pending; reviewer shard runner aggregates automatically after the last shard; reviewer shard runner fails closed without executor or pending shard
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-shard-runner.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: DeepSeek shard execution must go through the canonical claude-deepseek skill wrapper, keep shard prompts bounded, parse structured findings, and convert timeout or wrapper failure into reviewer findings instead of hanging the scheduler.
hardening_items.regression_test: claude deepseek shard command uses canonical wrapper script and shard limits; claude deepseek shard executor returns structured findings from stdout; claude deepseek shard executor converts timeouts into reviewer timeout findings
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/claude-deepseek-shard-executor.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Pending reviewer shards must be executable through a reusable CLI that writes workflow state, supports mock execution for deterministic tests, and fails closed on unreadable input.
hardening_items.regression_test: run-reviewer-shard CLI executes pending shards with mock executor; run-reviewer-shard CLI fails closed on unreadable input
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-shard-runner.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: When reviewer shard execution returns reviewer_timeout and provider-health recording is enabled, the runner must append reviewer_provider_health facts and schedule provider smoke unless a smoke result is already supplied.
hardening_items.regression_test: reviewer shard runner records provider health for timeout findings; run-reviewer-shard CLI records provider health on timeout findings; real DS reviewer shard run records pass without false provider health
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-shard-runner.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Reviewer shard runner must support a bounded loop that continues pending shards until aggregate, stops on provider-health recovery when configured, and exposes the same behavior through the scheduler CLI.
hardening_items.regression_test: reviewer shard loop runs pending shards until aggregate; reviewer shard loop stops after provider health recovery fact; run-reviewer-shard CLI can execute all pending shards with mock executor
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-shard-runner.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Autonomous continuation must re-evaluate completed reviewer shard aggregates from merged findings so stale pass/rerun evaluations cannot hide failed reviewer results or repeat already-recovered timeouts.
hardening_items.regression_test: reviewer shard aggregate failure overrides stale pass evaluation; reviewer shard aggregate pass clears stale rerun evaluation
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/autonomous-continuation.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Reviewer shard loop execution must be able to emit a replayable artifact that preserves input workflow state, runner parameters, shard runs, aggregate/provider-health stop facts, output workflow state, and run/cycle identity checks.
hardening_items.regression_test: reviewer shard loop run artifact is replay-valid; run-reviewer-shard CLI can execute all pending shards with mock executor
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-shard-runner.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Validated reviewer shard loop artifacts must be reusable through a scheduler CLI that emits closeout/continuation input without manual JSON assembly and fails closed on invalid artifacts.
hardening_items.regression_test: reviewer shard loop artifact prepares continuation input; run-reviewer-shard CLI can execute all pending shards with mock executor
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/reviewer-shard-runner.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: run_reviewer_scope_shard work packages must map to an auditable scheduler dispatch plan that runs reviewer shard loop, prepares continuation input, and executes autonomous closeout without manual command assembly.
hardening_items.regression_test: scheduler dispatch plan maps reviewer shard work packages to loop and closeout commands; scheduler dispatch plan fails closed without workflow state input path; scheduler dispatch plan CLI writes reviewer shard dispatch plan
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/scheduler-dispatch-plan.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Scheduler dispatch execution must validate plans, run only whitelisted npm scripts, respect step dependencies, stop on first failure, and emit scheduler-dispatch-run.v1 artifacts.
hardening_items.regression_test: scheduler dispatch runner validates allowed npm scripts; scheduler dispatch runner executes steps with injected executor; scheduler dispatch runner stops on step failure; run-scheduler-dispatch-plan CLI writes dry-run artifact
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/scheduler-dispatch-runner.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Deterministic reviewer mock behavior must be encoded in the scheduler dispatch plan, not injected secretly by the executor, so replay artifacts explain why external reviewers were bypassed.
hardening_items.regression_test: scheduler dispatch plan maps reviewer shard work packages to loop and closeout commands; scheduler dispatch plan CLI writes reviewer shard dispatch plan
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/scheduler-dispatch-plan.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Scheduler dispatch run artifacts must be recordable into workflow state and visible in PC/mobile workbench projection with status, phase, step counts, failed steps, and dry-run signal.
hardening_items.regression_test: scheduler dispatch run artifact records into workflow state; workbench projection exposes scheduler dispatch run status
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/scheduler-dispatch-runner.test.js test/workbench-projection.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Workbench projection fields are not complete until both PC and mobile shells render them and browser gates verify visible state without horizontal overflow.
hardening_items.enforcement_target: apps/workbench/desktop.html; apps/workbench/mobile.html; apps/workbench/workbench.js; apps/workbench/styles.css; tools/check-workbench-browser-events.mjs; test/workbench-shell.test.js
hardening_items.regression_test: workbench shell has separate desktop and mobile entries; workbench shell consumes projection json instead of logs
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-shell.test.js; npm run check:workbench:browser-events; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Scheduler dispatch run artifacts must be persistable through the workbench service with version/status/result shape checks, input_path ownership, and run/cycle identity validation before refreshing projection.
hardening_items.regression_test: scheduler dispatch run artifact recording rejects identity drift; workbench server records scheduler dispatch runs into workflow state input; projection source records scheduler dispatch runs
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/scheduler-dispatch-runner.test.js test/workbench-server.test.js test/workbench-projection-source.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Scheduler dispatch CLI runs must optionally write their artifact through the workbench service and fail closed when either execution or service writeback fails.
hardening_items.regression_test: run-scheduler-dispatch-plan CLI records scheduler dispatch run through workbench service
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-server.test.js; npm run check:scheduler-dispatch-writeback; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Scheduler dispatch service writeback must be verified through a deterministic e2e gate that renders updated scheduler_dispatch pass state in both PC and mobile workbench shells without horizontal overflow.
hardening_items.regression_test: check-scheduler-dispatch-writeback launches workbench service, runs scheduler dispatch dry-run writeback, and verifies PC/mobile rendered scheduler dispatch pass state
hardening_items.verification: npm run check:scheduler-dispatch-writeback; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Scheduler writeback strategy must be encoded in scheduler dispatch plans so autonomous dispatch can opt into service writeback without relying on manual CLI flags.
hardening_items.regression_test: scheduler dispatch plan maps reviewer shard work packages to loop and closeout commands; scheduler dispatch plan fails closed when service writeback lacks base url; scheduler dispatch plan CLI writes reviewer shard dispatch plan; check-scheduler-dispatch-writeback uses plan writeback config without manual writeback CLI flags
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/scheduler-dispatch-plan.test.js; npm run check:scheduler-dispatch-writeback; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Workbench service must be able to generate scheduler dispatch plans from projection history input_path and inject guarded service writeback context so operators do not manually assemble workflow paths, projection ids, or service URLs.
hardening_items.regression_test: workbench server creates scheduler dispatch plans from projection history input; workbench server rejects scheduler dispatch plans without workflow state input; workbench server rejects scheduler dispatch plan creation with unsafe host; projection source creates scheduler dispatch plans
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-server.test.js test/workbench-projection-source.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Workbench scheduler controls must run through the service, require dry-run for UI-triggered dispatch, persist scheduler dispatch artifacts, and update rendered state only from the returned projection.
hardening_items.enforcement_target: tools/workbench-server.mjs; apps/workbench/projection-source.js; apps/workbench/workbench.js; apps/workbench/desktop.html; apps/workbench/mobile.html; tools/check-workbench-browser-events.mjs; test/workbench-server.test.js; test/workbench-projection-source.test.js; test/workbench-shell.test.js; docs/contracts/AUTONOMOUS_CONTINUATION_CN.md
hardening_items.regression_test: workbench server runs guarded scheduler dispatch dry-run from projection history input; workbench server rejects non-dry-run scheduler dispatch from workbench control; projection source runs guarded scheduler dispatch; workbench shell consumes projection json instead of logs; scheduler_dispatch_click browser scenario
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/workbench-server.test.js test/workbench-projection-source.test.js test/workbench-shell.test.js; npm run check:workbench:browser-events; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Non-dry-run scheduler dispatch must be blocked unless an execution policy verifies explicit operator authorization, step limits, reviewer call budget, and provider cost mode before running or writing artifacts.
hardening_items.regression_test: scheduler dispatch control policy allows dry-run without execution authorization; scheduler dispatch control policy rejects non-dry-run without operator authorization; scheduler dispatch control policy allows mocked non-dry-run with explicit zero reviewer budget; scheduler dispatch control policy bounds non-mocked reviewer cost; workbench server rejects unauthorized non-dry-run scheduler dispatch from workbench control
hardening_items.verification: node --test test/scheduler-dispatch-policy.test.js test/workbench-server.test.js; npm run check:process-hardening; npm run check:closeout

hardening_items.invariant: Scheduler dispatch policy decisions must be recorded as durable workflow facts before execution or rejection, and PC/mobile projections must show policy status, execution mode, issue count, and the first rejection reason.
